Savoury Egg Tart with Spinach and Mushroom

chinese: Bō cài mógū rǔ dàn bǐng

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general quiche class (FOODON_03302051); no specific spinach-mushroom quiche subclass exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers quiche as a category; spinach-mushroom variant not individually coded. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code exists for spinach and mushroom quiche. No suitable parent code identified for this prepared dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard individual slice of spinach and mushroom quiche, approximately 150 g, as typically served at a café or coffee shop.

nutrient_source.title: Quiche, spinach, 1 piece (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for spinach quiche (FDC ID 172150 and related entries). Mushroom contribution estimated from USDA SR Legacy mushroom data (FDC ID 169251) and blended proportionally. Starbucks Singapore does not publish full nutrition panels; USDA composite used as best available approved-source estimate for a standard spinach-mushroom quiche.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for spinach and mushroom quiche. GI estimated at 45 based on analogous egg-and-pastry savoury tart / quiche entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Shortcrust pastry has a GI of approximately 59 (low-medium), but the high fat and protein content of the egg-cream custard filling substantially lowers the composite GI. A value of 45 (low GI) is defensible for a standard spinach-mushroom quiche. GL per 150 g serving = (45 × 15.2 × 1.5) / 100 = 10.3.

hypertension.guidance: A 150 g serving provides approximately 465 mg sodium (about 20% of the 2,300 mg daily limit), so individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods in the same meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load and pastry crust
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low GI (≈45) and moderate carbohydrate content (≈22.8 g per 150 g serving) make a single portion manageable for most people with Type 2 Diabetes, but the saturated fat from cream and cheese warrants attention given the increased cardiovascular risk in this population.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and cholesterol from cream, eggs, and cheese
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each 150 g serving delivers approximately 9.8 g saturated fat and 173 mg cholesterol; individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this as an occasional food and choose smaller portions to stay within saturated fat targets of less than 20 g per day.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Spinach and Mushroom Quiche is a classic Western savoury baked dish consisting of a shortcrust pastry shell filled with a rich egg-and-cream custard combined with sautéed spinach and mushrooms, typically seasoned with salt, pepper, and cheese such as Gruyère or cheddar. The dish originates from the Lorraine region of France and has become a staple of café and coffee-shop menus worldwide, including in Singapore where it is served at outlets such as Starbucks. The texture is firm yet creamy, with a flaky, buttery pastry base and a savoury, slightly earthy filling from the mushrooms and iron-rich spinach. Nutritionally, a standard 150 g serving provides approximately 327 kcal, 10.8 g protein, 22.2 g fat (of which 9.8 g is saturated), and 22.8 g carbohydrate. The dish is a source of calcium, vitamin A, vitamin B12, and iron from the dairy and vegetable components. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 45 (low GI), making it a relatively moderate-impact carbohydrate food. It is suitable for vegetarians but not vegans, and contains gluten, eggs, and dairy allergens. Clinically, the moderate sodium and saturated fat content require attention for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease, while the low GI and moderate carbohydrate load make it a manageable occasional choice for people with Type 2 Diabetes.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a quiche slice?
What is the GI of quiche?
Can I eat quiche if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional panel is published by Starbucks Singapore for this specific product. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy spinach quiche entries with mushroom contribution estimated from USDA SR Legacy mushroom data. The composite is a well-supported estimate from approved sources but is not an exact match to the Starbucks Singapore product formulation.
notes: Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy spinach quiche and mushroom data; exact Starbucks Singapore formulation is not publicly available. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all estimated from USDA SR Legacy composite data and are not confirmed from a Singapore-specific source. GI is an analogous estimate (45, low) based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for egg-and-pastry savoury tarts; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists. The food_group has been reclassified from the input value of 'Vegetables' to 'Dairy and eggs' as the dominant nutritional character of quiche is egg-and-dairy custard in a pastry shell. Cuisine reclassified from 'Coffee' (a venue tag) to 'Western' based on the dish's culinary origin. Halal status is not confirmed for the Starbucks Singapore product; is_halal set to false pending verification. No -1 sentinel values are used for core macros; all secondary nutrient -1 values have been replaced with USDA-estimated figures. All numeric fields are populated from approved sources.

improvement_actions_taken: Reclassified food_group from 'Vegetables' (input) to 'Dairy and eggs' to reflect the dominant egg-dairy-pastry nutritional character of quiche.
Reclassified cuisine from 'Coffee' (venue tag) to 'Western' based on culinary origin.
Populated all per_100g nutrient fields from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy spinach quiche and mushroom composite data; no -1 sentinel values remain for secondary nutrients.
Added GI estimate of 45 (low) with full analogous-estimate documentation referencing University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021.
Added Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names and aliases to satisfy Singaporean-context multilingual rule.
Added FoodOn and FoodEx2 ontology anchors with closest_parent match type and honest gap notes.
Added full health_context for Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and Cardiovascular Disease with specific clinical guidance.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list are perfectly consistent — all strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in aliases.
